6 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

What if you no longer had to train an XGBoost model every time you received a new dataset?
When people talk about AI breakthroughs, the conversation usually revolves around Large Language Models, image generation, or AI agents.
Very few people talk about tabular data.
Ironically, tabular data powers some of the most important machine learning applications in the world. Whether it’s predicting customer churn, detecting fraud, forecasting sales, estimating insurance risk, or approving loans, most enterprise AI systems still rely on rows and columns stored in spreadsheets or databases.
For years, models like XGBoost, Random Forest, and LightGBM have dominated this space because they consistently perform well on structured data. But anyone who has worked with them knows that building a good model isn’t as simple as calling. .fit().
Every new dataset usually means starting over.
You clean the data, engineer features, tune hyperparameters, train multiple models, compare results, and repeat the process until you achieve acceptable performance.
Google believes that workflow can be dramatically simplified.
